title: Chaetoseris cyanea
taxonomy: Chaetoseris cyanea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Chaetoseris, a group of plants native to the mountainous regions of China and Central Asia. Chaetoseris cyanea is further classified under the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary history and relationships among various plant species.

intro: Chaetoseris cyanea, also known as Blue Spike Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Chaetoseris cyanea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Chaetoseris cyanea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
